_meta.description: Business DQ context for customer_profiles table — upload this in the New Workflow > Business Context file picker

table_description: Core customer master table for a retail banking platform. Each row represents one registered customer. Used by credit risk, KYC compliance, product eligibility, and marketing teams. Data freshness SLA is 24 hours during business hours.

customer_id.description: Unique customer identifier. Must be globally unique — no duplicates allowed.

email.description: Primary contact email address. Must be unique per customer.

phone_number.description: International E.164 phone number including country code.

date_of_birth.description: Customer date of birth. Customers must be at least 18 years old at registration. Maximum age enforced at 120 years.

country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of customer residence. Must be exactly 2 uppercase letters AND in the supported countries list.

credit_score.description: FICO-style credit score. Standard range is 300–850. Scores below 580 are considered poor, 580–669 fair, 670–739 good, 740–799 very good, 800+ exceptional.
constraints: nullable — NULL means not yet scored
credit_score.business_rule: A credit_score of NULL is acceptable (unscored customer). Any non-null score must be in [300, 850].

account_balance.description: Current account balance in USD. Cannot be negative for standard accounts.

account_status.description: Current lifecycle status of the customer account.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when the customer record was created.
created_at.sla: Must be within 24 hours of current time for active monitoring. Last record is from 2024-11-03 — table is ~13,480 hours stale.

loan_amount.description: Outstanding loan balance in USD. NULL means no active loan.
